mechanism: The shift to USD-denominated LNG creates a structural channel constraining ECB policy space: gas price spikes simultaneously weaken EUR (current account deterioration) and boost CPI (energy pass-through), creating a double constraint that limits ECB's ability to ease independently of the Fed.
regime_dependence: Holds as long as Europe depends on USD-denominated LNG (~120bcm/year). Would weaken if Europe diversifies LNG sourcing or returns to pipeline gas. Strongest during periods of gas price volatility.
